data.title: Houston Airport Warns of Extended Wait Times Amid TSA Checkpoint Closures
data.description: Passengers at George Bush Intercontinental Airport in Houston are facing potential wait times of over four hours due to the closure of several TSA checkpoints. Bloomberg posted on X that the airport has also suspended all PreCheck and CLEAR operations, leading to significant delays for travelers. The shutdowns have caused disruptions in passenger flow, prompting airport officials to advise travelers to plan accordingly and arrive early to accommodate the extended wait times. The situation underscores the importance of efficient security operations in maintaining smooth airport procedures.

data.title: Brazilian Court Allows Bolsonaro to Serve Sentence Under House Arrest
data.description: A Brazilian Supreme Court judge has decided that former President Jair Bolsonaro can serve his sentence for plotting a coup under house arrest. Bloomberg posted on X, citing local website UOL, that this decision permits Bolsonaro to leave prison due to concerns over his deteriorating health.

data.title: AI TRENDS | OpenAI Introduces Prompt-Based Safety Strategy for Teen Protection
data.description: OpenAI has unveiled a new safety strategy aimed at assisting developers in creating age-appropriate protective measures for teenagers. According to Jin10, the initiative focuses on utilizing prompt-based techniques to enhance the safety and suitability of AI applications for younger users. This development is part of OpenAI's ongoing efforts to ensure that AI technologies are used responsibly and safely, particularly in contexts involving minors. The strategy is designed to guide developers in implementing safeguards that align with the needs and vulnerabilities of teenage users, promoting a safer digital environment.

data.title: U.S. Treasury Issues General License for Venezuelan Government Official Travel
data.description: The U.S. Treasury Department has issued a general license concerning official travel by the Venezuelan government, according to Jin10. This development comes amid ongoing geopolitical tensions and economic challenges facing Venezuela. The license aims to facilitate diplomatic engagements and official visits, potentially easing some of the restrictions previously imposed on Venezuelan officials. This move may signal a shift in U.S. policy towards Venezuela, as the two countries navigate complex diplomatic relations. The implications of this license on the broader geopolitical landscape remain to be seen.

data.title: U.S. Treasuries Decline Amid Middle East Conflict Concerns
data.description: U.S. Treasuries experienced a decline as investors showed little interest in an auction of two-year notes. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting concerns that the ongoing conflict in the Middle East could trigger a resurgence in inflation driven by oil prices. The auction's lackluster performance reflects investor apprehension about potential economic impacts stemming from the geopolitical situation. The situation underscores the market's sensitivity to global events and their potential to influence inflationary pressures.

data.title: Israel Defense Forces: Over 120 Ammunition Used in Strikes on Regime Targets
data.description: The Israel Defense Forces (IDF) announced that they have conducted strikes using more than 120 pieces of ammunition on various regime targets. According to Jin10, the targets included ballistic missile launch sites, weapons manufacturing facilities, and defense systems associated with the Iranian regime. The IDF's actions are part of ongoing military operations in the region.

data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Drops Below 69,000 USDT with a 2.74% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Mar 24, 2026, 17:45 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has dropped below 69,000 USDT and is now trading at 68,968.679688 USDT, with a narrowed 2.74% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: France's President Macron Urges Compliance with Infrastructure Protection Amid Conflicts
data.description: French President Emmanuel Macron has emphasized the necessity for all parties involved in conflicts to strictly adhere to the protection of civilian and energy infrastructure. According to Jin10, Macron also called on Iran to restore freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz. Additionally, he highlighted the urgency of preventing further escalation in Lebanon.

data.title: Executives Convene in Caracas Amid Economic Discussions
data.description: Dozens of hedge-fund and oil-company executives are gathering this week in Caracas to discuss economic strategies and opportunities. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the significance of these meetings in the context of global economic shifts. The discussions are expected to focus on investment prospects and the impact of geopolitical factors on the oil industry. Participants aim to explore potential collaborations and assess market trends that could influence future business decisions. This gathering underscores the importance of Caracas as a hub for economic dialogue and strategic planning among industry leaders.

data.title: Eureka Labs Secures $6.7 Million in Seed Funding and Launches Programmable Ethereum Blocks
data.description: Eureka Labs has successfully raised $6.7 million in a seed funding round, marking a significant milestone for the company. According to NS3.AI, the funds will be used to further develop and introduce what Eureka Labs describes as programmable Ethereum blocks. The company has established itself as a prominent builder on the Ethereum network, currently ranking as the fourth-largest by the number of blocks constructed, holding approximately a 1.5% market share. This development highlights Eureka Labs' growing influence in the blockchain space and its commitment to advancing Ethereum's capabilities.

data.title: Netflix's BTS Comeback Show Attracts Global Audience of 18.4 Million
data.description: Netflix's recent BTS comeback show garnered significant attention, drawing 18.4 million viewers worldwide. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the strong demand for live events featuring high-profile talent. This viewership underscores the global appeal of BTS and the continued interest in real-time entertainment offerings.

data.title: Bitcoin Core Enhances Security Measures to Mitigate Supply-Chain Risks
data.description: Bitcoin Core's build system is structured to allow independent contributors to reproduce and verify the binaries distributed on bitcoincore.org. According to NS3.AI, the project employs Guix, a tool that helps ensure reproducibility and security. By avoiding auto-updates and continuously reducing external dependencies, Bitcoin Core aims to minimize supply-chain risks, enhancing the overall security of its software.

data.title: Swiss Central Bank Governor: Prepared for Negative Interest Rates, But Threshold Higher
data.description: The Swiss Central Bank is ready to implement negative interest rates if necessary, according to its governor. According to Jin10, the governor emphasized that while the bank is prepared for such a move, the threshold for lowering rates into negative territory has become higher. This statement reflects the bank's cautious approach to monetary policy amid global economic uncertainties. The governor's remarks highlight the challenges central banks face in balancing economic growth and inflation control. The Swiss Central Bank continues to monitor economic indicators closely to determine the appropriate course of action.

data.title: Dollar's Rally May Slow Amid Shifting Market Focus, Says Goldman Sachs
data.description: The dollar's recent rally, which began with the onset of the conflict in Iran, may face a slowdown. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting insights from foreign exchange strategists at Goldman Sachs. They suggest that if currency and rates markets begin to prioritize growth concerns over the inflationary impacts of the conflict, the dollar's upward momentum could diminish. This shift in focus could alter the current trajectory of the dollar's performance in the global market.

data.title: Rachel Reeves Indicates Limited Energy Support Package
data.description: Rachel Reeves has suggested that a comprehensive energy support package may not be forthcoming. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that while Reeves has not completely dismissed the idea, she remains non-committal on the specifics. Reeves appears to be taking a cautious approach, opting to delay detailed announcements as she assesses the situation further. Her stance reflects a strategic decision to manage expectations while navigating the complexities of energy policy.

data.title: Travelers Question Optimal Arrival Time at Airports
data.description: Travelers are increasingly concerned about the best time to arrive at airports to ensure a smooth journey. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the dilemma faced by passengers who are unsure whether arriving five hours early is necessary or if four hours would suffice. This uncertainty stems from varying airport security procedures and the potential for unexpected delays. As travel continues to evolve, passengers are advised to stay informed about airport guidelines and plan accordingly to avoid missing flights.

data.title: Estée Lauder's Potential Deal with Puig Faces Challenges
data.description: Wall Street analysts are evaluating the potential benefits and risks for Estée Lauder in pursuing a deal with Puig. Bloomberg posted on X that while the transaction could offer significant advantages, the timing poses challenges as Estée Lauder is currently undergoing its own turnaround process. The complexities of executing such a deal during this period may outweigh the potential gains, according to market observers.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Arm to Sell Proprietary Chips, Targets AI Market
data.description: Arm, known for licensing technology to semiconductor manufacturers, is set to sell its own chips for the first time, aiming to capitalize on the substantial spending in the AI device sector. According to Jin10, Arm announced at an event in San Francisco on Tuesday that Meta will be the primary customer for its new AGI CPU chip. The product is expected to feature up to 136 cores, a measure of processing power, and will consume 300 watts of power. Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC) will handle the production of these chips. Following the announcement, Arm's U.S. stock surged to an intraday high.

data.title: Swiss Central Bank Governor: Swiss Franc a Safe Haven in Uncertain Times
data.description: The Swiss franc is considered a safe haven during periods of uncertainty, according to the Swiss Central Bank Governor. According to Jin10, the governor emphasized the currency's stability and reliability, which attract investors seeking security amid global economic fluctuations. The Swiss franc's reputation as a refuge asset is bolstered by Switzerland's strong economic fundamentals and prudent monetary policies. This perception often leads to increased demand for the currency during turbulent times, reinforcing its status as a preferred choice for risk-averse investors.

data.title: Czech Police Arrest Suspects in Military Company Arson Case
data.description:
Czech authorities have detained three individuals suspected of setting fire to a military company. According to RTHK, the suspects include Czech and American nationals, with two apprehended in the Czech Republic and one in Slovakia. Authorities are collaborating with international partners to pursue other individuals involved.

The incident occurred last Friday, with Czech media reporting that the LPP military company's factory and administrative building in the eastern city of Pardubice were allegedly set on fire. While some office facilities were damaged, no casualties were reported. The company is known for producing drones supplied to Ukraine.

Following the incident, an anti-Israel organization claimed responsibility for the arson, accusing LPP of manufacturing weapons for Israel. However, LPP has denied any connections with Israeli defense companies.
